ai_strategy_lead.name: Nitesh Chawla
ai_strategy_lead.title: Lucy Family Director for Data and AI Academic Strategy; Director, Data, AI, and Computing Initiative; Founding Director, Lucy Family Institute for Data & Society

documents.title: Data, AI, and Computing Initiative (Notre Dame 2033 Strategic Framework)
Generative AI Task Force (GAIT) Report and Recommendations
Spring 2025 AI at the University of Notre Dame Update

ai_strategy.summary: Notre Dame's AI posture is framed by its 'Notre Dame 2033' Strategic Framework, within which the Data, AI, and Computing Initiative (launched fall 2025) is one of five priority areas. Led by Nitesh Chawla as founding director with managing director Michele Martin, the initiative funded 19 faculty-led working groups (announced February 2026) across five priority tracks: AI-enabled science and engineering, foundational AI and next-gen computing, Responsible/Inclusive/Safe/Empowering (RISE) AI, computational social science, and digital humanities. The strategy is distinct in pairing foundational and applied research with an explicit Catholic, faith-informed ethics overlay via the $50.8M Lilly Endowment-funded DELTA Network at the Institute for Ethics and the Common Good. Operational execution is organized through AI@ND (portal), the Generative AI Task Force (GAIT), and a 20-member AI Innovation Council, with the Lucy Family Institute serving as the interdisciplinary research hub and the Center for Research Computing as the compute backbone.

key_provisions: Generative AI use governed by the Undergraduate Academic Code of Honor — using AI in violation of an instructor's articulated policy is an Honor Code violation
Faculty hold authority to define acceptable AI use in their courses; policy explicitly requires syllabus-level articulation
Five-level Generative AI Acceptable Use Scale (Prohibited, Limited Use, Moderate Use, Full Use with Documentation, Full Use with Human Oversight) provided as syllabus-writing framework

Approved/licensed AI tools require SSO authentication and are routed through contractually protected vendor instances (Google Gemini/NotebookLM under existing Google EDU licenses, pursuing ChatGPT Edu contract, GitHub Copilot centrally managed via OIT)
Data governance review (led by CIO Livingston) is simplifying Notre Dame's four-tier data classification (public/internal/sensitive/highly sensitive) with an AI lens
Campus communicators governed by separate On Message generative AI guidelines for institutional content
Guidance distinguishes permitted educational uses (idea generation, study guides, concept clarification) from prohibited unattributed authorship
ai_policy.summary: Notre Dame operates a comprehensive, layered policy: a university-wide student generative AI policy anchored in the Honor Code (effective August 2023, updated Fall 2024), a 5-level 'Generative AI Acceptable Use Scale' for syllabi, faculty authority to set course-level rules, approved/vetted tools tied to SSO and contractually protected enterprise instances, and campus-communicator guidelines for institutional messaging. A parallel data-governance review (led by CIO Jane Livingston) is modernizing Notre Dame's long-standing four-tier data classification scheme through an AI lens, working through the information governance committee, information security team, and Office of General Counsel.

bodies.name: Generative AI Task Force (GAIT)
bodies.scope: Interdisciplinary working group co-chaired by the CIO and VP for Research that assesses generative AI benefits and risks, guides community use, and recommends policy and governance. Produced initial report and recommendations.

bodies.name: AI Innovation Council
bodies.scope: Cross-disciplinary 20-member body chaired by the VP for IT and the Director of AI Enablement. Meets monthly to advise the AI Enablement Team on implementing GAIT recommendations. Members span OIT, Hesburgh Libraries, Mendoza, Research, Learning, Lucy Family Institute, and the Institute for Ethics and the Common Good.

bodies.name: Data, AI, and Computing Initiative Executive Committee
bodies.scope: Executive committee of 11 academic leaders (including deans of Engineering, Science, Arts and Letters, and Mendoza) chaired by Nitesh Chawla to implement Notre Dame's university-wide Data, AI, and Computing Initiative.

bodies.name: Task Force on Data, AI and Computing Integration in and for Curriculum and Learning
bodies.scope: Task force established under the Data, AI, and Computing Initiative to integrate data and AI into Notre Dame's curriculum and learning enterprise.

bodies.name: Information Governance Committee
bodies.scope: OIT-led body overseeing data stewardship and classification; actively revising Notre Dame's four-tier data classification framework through an AI lens alongside the Office of General Counsel.

bodies.name: AI Enablement Team
bodies.scope: Operational team within OIT, directed by Brandon Rich (Director of AI Enablement), that delivers the institution's AI tool portfolio, pilots, and the AI Development Platform.

bodies.name: Lab for AI in Teaching and Learning (LAITL)
bodies.scope: Faculty-facing teaching-and-learning lab (founded 2025, directed by Alex Ambrose at the Kaneb Center) that convenes faculty, postdocs, and undergraduate AI coaches to advance AI literacy, run the Teaching Well with AI Academy, and administer AI mini-grants.

governance_structure.summary: Notre Dame runs a multi-body governance stack: a CIO/VP-Research-co-chaired Generative AI Task Force that produced foundational recommendations; a 20-member AI Innovation Council that meets monthly to advise on implementation; a strategic-framework-level Data, AI, and Computing Initiative with its own executive committee and curriculum task force; an OIT Information Governance Committee overseeing data classification; and a dedicated Lab for AI in Teaching and Learning inside Notre Dame Learning. The Tech Ethics Lab (Notre Dame-IBM) adds an external-facing research-ethics layer.

initiatives.title: Data, AI, and Computing Initiative (Notre Dame 2033 Strategic Framework)
initiatives.description: University-wide strategic initiative launched fall 2025 as one of five priority areas in the Notre Dame 2033 Strategic Framework. Led by Nitesh Chawla as founding director and Michele Martin as managing director; funded 19 faculty-led working groups announced February 2026 across five priority tracks: AI-enabled science and engineering; foundational AI and next-gen computing; Responsible, Inclusive, Safe, and Empowering (RISE) AI; computational social science; and digital humanities.
initiatives.significance: Establishes a durable, provost-level strategic home for AI with five-year leadership terms and a permanent cross-college executive committee, embedding AI as a pillar of Notre Dame's institutional identity rather than a transient initiative.

investment.funding_source: Institutional (Office of the Provost; University Strategic Framework)
investment.description: Five-year leadership terms for faculty directors; university-wide call funded 19 faculty-led working groups.

partners: Lucy Family Institute for Data & Society
collaborations.description: Cross-campus alignment spanning colleges, institutes, and research administration.

initiatives.policy_implications: Formalizes AI as a top-level institutional priority with dedicated governance and resource allocation.

initiatives.title: DELTA Network — Faith-Based Ethical Formation for a World of Powerful AI
initiatives.description: Notre Dame's Institute for Ethics and the Common Good (ECG), directed by philosophy professor Meghan Sullivan, received a $50.8 million grant from Lilly Endowment Inc. on December 19, 2025 to develop and scale the DELTA Network — a Christian-inspired, five-pillar ethical framework (Dignity, Embodiment, Love, Transcendence, Agency) intended to be accessible across faith perspectives. Plans include young-adult programs, Silicon Valley and Northeast hubs, and resources for scholars, religious leaders, tech professionals, educators, and journalists.
initiatives.significance: Largest grant from a private foundation in Notre Dame's history and the most ambitious faith-based AI ethics program in U.S. higher education, positioning Notre Dame as the national anchor for religious-tradition-grounded AI ethics discourse.

investment.description: Largest private-foundation grant in Notre Dame's history, following an October 2024 $539,000 planning grant.

partners: IBM (via Notre Dame-IBM Technology Ethics Lab)
collaborations.description: DELTA leverages Notre Dame's existing 10-year Tech Ethics Lab partnership with IBM Research.

initiatives.policy_implications: Introduces a values-based framework (DELTA) intended to inform syllabi, corporate ethics boards, and public policy; complements the Honor Code policy with a deeper ethical scaffolding.

initiatives.title: Generative AI Task Force (GAIT) and AI Innovation Council
initiatives.description: GAIT is an interdisciplinary task force co-chaired by Jane Livingston (VP-IT/CIO) and Jeffrey Rhoads (VP for Research) that produced Notre Dame's foundational generative AI recommendations. The 20-member AI Innovation Council — co-chaired by the CIO and Director of AI Enablement Brandon Rich — meets monthly to implement those recommendations and drive the AI tool portfolio.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates bi-cameral governance (research + IT) combined with monthly operational cadence, a replicable model for mid-size private R1s.

investment.description: Staff time, facilitation, and report production; published GAIT Report and Recommendations.

partners: Institute for Ethics and the Common Good
collaborations.description: Cross-campus representation across schools, research, libraries, and ethics.

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates the mechanism through which all acceptable-use policies, approved-tool lists, and vendor contracts are vetted.

initiatives.title: Enterprise Google Gemini and NotebookLM Rollout
initiatives.description: Notre Dame rolled out Google Gemini and NotebookLM to staff and faculty in January 2025 and extended access to students in March 2025 (announced by OIT on March 18, 2025). Tools are included in existing Google EDU licenses with contractually extended data-privacy protections; faculty and staff users get Gmail/Calendar/Drive integration.
initiatives.significance: Campus-wide deployment of enterprise generative AI at zero incremental license cost, reached through contractual data-protection terms — a template many private institutions are emulating.

investment.description: No incremental license cost; negotiated contractual data-privacy extension for education users.

collaborations.description: Existing Google Workspace for Education contract extended to cover Gemini and NotebookLM.

tools_and_vendors.name: Gemini
tools_and_vendors.role: Campus-wide generative AI assistant with Workspace integration

initiatives.policy_implications: All campus users have a vetted, SSO-protected AI option with institutional data protections; reduces shadow-AI usage.

initiatives.title: ChatGPT Team/Edu Pilot and Enterprise Contract
initiatives.description: Summer 2024 through January 2025 ChatGPT Team pilot involved 250+ faculty and staff exploring campus use cases with institutional data. OIT subsequently pursued an OpenAI ChatGPT Edu contract for centralized, managed ChatGPT accounts with contractual data protection and SSO.
initiatives.significance: Concrete transition from a bounded pilot to a long-term managed enterprise contract is the standard maturity path many R1s are on; Notre Dame has sequenced it cleanly.

investment.description: Paid Team pilot transitioning to Edu enterprise contract.

collaborations.description: Vendor pilot with data-protection and SSO terms moving to ChatGPT Edu contract.

initiatives.policy_implications: Provides a managed, FERPA-aware alternative to consumer ChatGPT for campus use.

initiatives.title: GitHub Copilot Centrally Managed Developer Rollout
initiatives.description: Following evaluation by campus developers in Hesburgh Libraries, OIT, and the Mendoza College of Business, Notre Dame centralized GitHub Copilot subscriptions via OIT, making AI-assisted code generation available to faculty and staff developers.
initiatives.significance: Extends enterprise AI governance into the developer toolchain, an often-overlooked shadow-AI vector.

investment.description: Centrally funded and managed developer licenses.

collaborations.description: Enterprise agreement for Copilot developer seats.

initiatives.policy_implications: Brings developer AI use under institutional license and acceptable-use policy.

initiatives.title: Notre Dame Teaching Well with AI Academy (NDTWAA) and Lab for AI in Teaching and Learning (LAITL)
initiatives.description: LAITL, founded 2025 within Notre Dame Learning's Kaneb Center and directed by Alex Ambrose, runs the Teaching Well with AI Academy (NDTWAA) — a faculty development program that trained 25+ faculty from across colleges in its first cohort (spring 2025). LAITL also offers AI coaching (delivered by trained undergraduate coaches), an AI Mini-Grant Program, and a faculty-assignment repository.
initiatives.significance: A fully staffed, named faculty development program for AI pedagogy is rare at mid-size private R1s; LAITL's use of undergraduate AI coaches is a distinctive peer-learning model.

investment.description: Dedicated lab staffing, undergraduate coach program, and AI mini-grants.

collaborations.description: Cross-unit partnership inside Notre Dame Learning.

initiatives.policy_implications: Operationalizes the five-level Acceptable Use Scale through faculty training and syllabus design support.

initiatives.title: AI Alliance Founding Membership (IBM, Meta, and 50+ Partners)
initiatives.description: On December 5, 2023 Notre Dame joined IBM, Meta, and a global coalition of technology developers, researchers, and universities (including Cornell, Dartmouth, UC Berkeley, Yale, Illinois, IIT Bombay, Imperial College London, Tokyo, and others) to launch the AI Alliance, focused on open, safe, responsible AI development, benchmarks, and education.
initiatives.significance: One of a handful of U.S. universities to be a founding member of the Alliance, giving Notre Dame a seat at open-source AI standard-setting alongside frontier-lab stakeholders.

investment.description: In-kind research collaboration; Alliance working groups on benchmarks, tools, safety, and education.

collaborations.description: Global consortium advancing open, responsible AI.

initiatives.policy_implications: Aligns Notre Dame with open-model advocacy and responsible-AI benchmarks used across the field.

initiatives.title: Notre Dame-IBM Technology Ethics Lab (10-year, $20M)
initiatives.description: Launched in 2020 with a 10-year, $20 million IBM commitment, the Notre Dame-IBM Tech Ethics Lab produces applied, interdisciplinary research on ethical AI. In 2025 it published the BenchmarkCards framework — a catalog of datasets, benchmarks, and mitigations now incorporated into IBM's Risk Atlas Nexus — and it convened a Responsible AI in Finance event in 2025.
initiatives.significance: One of the largest sustained industry-academic commitments to tech ethics; produced infrastructure (BenchmarkCards) adopted into IBM's production governance tooling.

investment.description: IBM 10-year, $20M commitment; annual CFP supports external researchers.

collaborations.description: Joint research lab with open research-grant process.

tools_and_vendors.name: BenchmarkCards
tools_and_vendors.role: AI governance benchmarking framework adopted into IBM's Risk Atlas Nexus

initiatives.policy_implications: Produces reusable governance artifacts (BenchmarkCards, Risk Atlas entries) that shape industry AI risk management.

initiatives.title: Lucy Family Institute for Data & Society (Founded with $25M Lumpkins Gift)
initiatives.description: Founded in December 2019 with a $25 million gift from alumnus Robert Lumpkins and wife Sara, the Lucy Family Institute advances data-driven and AI convergence research, translational solutions, and education. It houses eight labs including the AI Trust and Reliability (AITAR) Lab, Foundation Models and Applications Lab, and Human-Centered Responsible AI (HRAI) Lab, plus the integrated Center for Network and Data Science and Center for Social Science Research.
initiatives.significance: Serves as the university's translational AI hub, pairing foundational research with applied social-impact work; the AITAR and HRAI labs give Notre Dame dedicated investment in responsible AI methodology.

investment.description: $25M founding gift plus subsequent programmatic funding.

partners: Center for Network and Data Science
collaborations.description: Integrated multi-lab research institute.

initiatives.policy_implications: Provides the organizational home for responsible AI and AI-for-social-good research under Notre Dame's Catholic mission.

initiatives.title: Scientific AI (SAI) Initiative
initiatives.description: Interdisciplinary research initiative focused on AI-enabled science and engineering — integrating AI with partial differential equations, laboratory automation and robotic experimentation, and polymer-data research. Runs a Spring 2026 graduate fellowship program for PhD students integrating AI with domain research, and partners with Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile and the University of Puerto Rico on a winter school.
initiatives.significance: A domain-specific AI-for-science initiative with international collaboration and a funded graduate fellowship — the seed of a broader AI-for-discovery research cluster.

investment.description: Funded graduate fellowships and international workshops.

collaborations.description: Joint winter school on scientific AI.

initiatives.title: RISE AI Conference (Inaugural 2025)
initiatives.description: The inaugural RISE (Responsible, Inclusive, Safe, Empowering) AI Conference, hosted October 6-8, 2025 by the Lucy Family Institute, drew 330+ participants from 16+ countries, 55 companies, and 38 universities. Six research tracks; selected global researchers from 170+ submitted abstracts; co-located Health AI Forum on rural health.
initiatives.significance: Establishes Notre Dame as a convener of the human-centered AI research community and gives its RISE framework a dedicated annual venue.

investment.description: Conference programming and speaker honoraria.

collaborations.description: Global convening on responsible AI.

initiatives.policy_implications: Informs Notre Dame's RISE framework and contributes to broader public-sector AI policy discourse.

initiatives.title: AWS/NEON Responsible and Ethical AI Course for Title I High Schools (Arnie Chatbot)
initiatives.description: AWS-funded grant (AWS credits) supporting Notre Dame's pilot Responsible and Ethical AI course delivered through the National Education Opportunity Network (NEON) to approximately 190 scholars in Title I and Title I-eligible high schools starting fall 2025. Students earn college credit from Notre Dame; the course uses 'Arnie,' a custom chatbot built by Notre Dame OIT and powered by Amazon Nova Lite on Amazon Bedrock in a secure sandbox.
initiatives.significance: One of the most concrete R1 commitments to AI access equity — pairing dual-enrollment credit with a university-built, cloud-secured chatbot deployed to low-income high schools.

investment.description: AWS credits underwrite compute for the Arnie chatbot; Notre Dame provides course tuition credit.

collaborations.description: Corporate, nonprofit, and K-12 partnership delivering college-credit AI education to Title I students.

tools_and_vendors.name: Arnie Chatbot
tools_and_vendors.vendor: Notre Dame OIT / AWS Nova Lite on Bedrock
tools_and_vendors.role: Custom course chatbot in secure sandbox environment

initiatives.policy_implications: Extends Notre Dame's data-privacy and secure-sandbox posture into K-12 dual enrollment and sets a model for equitable AI pre-college access.

initiatives.title: Student Advising AI Chatbot Pilot
initiatives.description: Notre Dame is developing an AI-powered chatbot — in partnership with student government, the Center for University Advising, and the Registrar's Office — to answer student questions about undergraduate degree opportunities, grounded in authoritative university data.
initiatives.significance: Classic enterprise-AI-for-student-services deployment built from validated data rather than public-web scraping.

investment.description: Pilot build with internal resources.

collaborations.description: Multi-unit partnership grounding the bot in university data.

initiatives.policy_implications: Advising-grade accuracy requirements drive data-governance investment.

initiatives.title: Praxis AI Classroom Pilot
initiatives.description: 25 instructors and 31 classes participated in a Praxis AI classroom pilot, generating 10,000+ prompts for evaluation; pilot extended into spring 2025.
initiatives.significance: Evidence Notre Dame is willing to test classroom-specific AI tutoring platforms at scale rather than relying solely on horizontal chat tools.

investment.description: Licensing and evaluation costs for a multi-section pilot.

collaborations.description: Vendor pilot across 31 sections.

initiatives.title: AI Development Platform (Internal Secure Sandbox)
initiatives.description: OIT-built internal AI Development Platform launched January 2025 and targeted for completion in spring 2025 — a secure sandbox for campus developers to build AI applications with contractually protected data.
initiatives.significance: Gives Notre Dame application developers a first-party platform rather than forcing them onto consumer APIs that can't hold institutional data.

investment.description: Platform engineering staffing and cloud infrastructure.

collaborations.description: Integrates multiple vetted model providers behind institutional controls.

initiatives.policy_implications: Reduces shadow-AI risk by providing a compliant internal build target.

initiatives.title: Data Governance and Classification Modernization
initiatives.description: CIO Jane Livingston is leading a consulting-supported review of Notre Dame's four-tier data classification (public, internal, sensitive, highly sensitive) dating to 2013, working with the Information Governance Committee, information security team, and Office of General Counsel to simplify and modernize the framework through an AI lens.
initiatives.significance: Real example of institutional data-governance modernization explicitly driven by generative AI — a necessary prerequisite for defensible enterprise AI use.

investment.description: Consulting spend plus internal cross-functional review.

collaborations.description: External advisory partner supporting the review.

initiatives.policy_implications: Will reshape data handling, vendor contracting, and AI tool approval processes.

initiatives.title: Privacy Sandbox (GPT-4 Persona-Based Consumer Research Tool)
initiatives.description: Notre Dame researchers developed a Chrome browser plug-in that replaces real participant data with GPT-4-generated personas so consumers can understand online data exploitation, contributing open research to the consumer-privacy toolkit.
initiatives.significance: A public-good AI research artifact aimed at non-academic end users, shipped as an open plugin.

investment.description: Faculty-led research plug-in.

collaborations.description: Public-facing research output.

tools_and_vendors.name: Privacy Sandbox
tools_and_vendors.role: GPT-4-powered persona generation for consumer privacy research

initiatives.title: AI Distortion of Historical Information Policy Brief
initiatives.description: The Lucy Family Institute released a January 2026 policy brief warning that AI systems systematically distort historical information, posing threats to democratic discourse and participation nationally and internationally, and calling for mitigation action.
initiatives.significance: Public-policy contribution from the Lucy Family Institute operationalizing Notre Dame's responsible-AI voice into concrete regulatory recommendations.

investment.description: Research and publication cost.

collaborations.description: Institute-internal policy output.

initiatives.policy_implications: Advocates for regulatory and design-level intervention to prevent AI historical distortion.

initiatives.title: MS in Business Analytics (Mendoza, STEM-designated) and AI Coursework Portfolio
initiatives.description: Mendoza College of Business offers a STEM-designated one-year MS in Business Analytics (31 credit hours, locations in South Bend and Chicago) covering data management, predictive analytics, machine learning, and emerging AI issues; complemented by undergraduate offerings including a data science minor, the Idzik Computing & Digital Technologies (CDT) minor, and dedicated AI courses (Language Processing in Practice, CSE 40171 AI and Society).
initiatives.significance: Provides the workforce-development layer of Notre Dame's AI strategy — a professional master's plus liberal-arts-friendly minors.

investment.description: Dedicated degree program, faculty, and two-city delivery model.

collaborations.description: Industry engagement via Chicago cohort.

computing_infrastructure.summary: The Center for Research Computing (CRC) operates Notre Dame's research supercomputing infrastructure, providing HPC clusters, GPU resources, grid networks, and storage, with dedicated HPC staff support including GPU-accelerated molecular dynamics expertise. CRC supports AI workloads for faculty, researchers, and external organizations. Specific node counts and GPU totals are not publicly enumerated.

regulatory_context.summary: Indiana has published a State of Indiana Policy: Artificial Intelligence (v1.1, December 2024) governing state agency use and established an AI Task Force running through 2027. Legislative activity in 2025 (HB 1296) focused on K-12 AI policy guidelines and AI-detector protections but did not directly regulate private higher education. Notre Dame is a private, non-system institution and is not covered by a system-level AI policy. Federal Executive Order 14365 (2026) signals federal preemption trends that may shape future institutional policy.

narrative_summary:
The University of Notre Dame has moved in roughly two years from scattered AI exploration to one of the most structurally developed AI postures in Catholic higher education. Its strategy is anchored by three architectural pieces: (1) the university-wide Data, AI, and Computing Initiative — one of five priorities in the Notre Dame 2033 Strategic Framework, launched fall 2025 under founding director Nitesh Chawla, now driving 19 faculty-led working groups across foundational AI, AI-for-science, RISE (Responsible, Inclusive, Safe, Empowering) AI, computational social science, and digital humanities; (2) a mature, bi-cameral governance stack — the Generative AI Task Force co-chaired by the CIO and VP for Research, a 20-member AI Innovation Council meeting monthly, plus the Information Governance Committee and the Lab for AI in Teaching and Learning (LAITL); and (3) a distinctive faith-based ethics layer led by the Institute for Ethics and the Common Good, which received the largest private-foundation grant in Notre Dame's history — $50.8 million from Lilly Endowment in December 2025 — to scale the DELTA (Dignity, Embodiment, Love, Transcendence, Agency) framework.

Operationally, Notre Dame has executed a disciplined enterprise AI rollout: Google Gemini and NotebookLM campus-wide under existing EDU licenses with contractually extended data protection, a 250-person ChatGPT Team pilot transitioning to ChatGPT Edu, centrally managed GitHub Copilot for developers, an internal AI Development Platform sandbox, and a Praxis AI classroom tutoring pilot across 31 sections. Research output is channeled through the Lucy Family Institute (founded 2019 on a $25M Lumpkins gift, housing 8 AI-focused labs including AITAR, HRAI, and FMAL), the Notre Dame-IBM Technology Ethics Lab (10-year, $20M), the Scientific AI Initiative, and founding membership in the global AI Alliance with IBM, Meta, and 50+ partners. Equity work centers on the AWS-funded NEON Responsible and Ethical AI course delivering college-credit AI education to ~190 Title I high school students through the custom Arnie chatbot on Amazon Nova Lite / Bedrock. Policy is comprehensive — Honor Code-grounded for students, a five-level syllabus Acceptable Use Scale for faculty, an ongoing data-classification modernization, and dedicated campus-communicator guidance. Remaining gaps: no explicit chief AI officer role, no public GPU/node inventory, and no disclosed total AI investment envelope.

gaps_acknowledged: No published Chief AI Officer role; Nitesh Chawla serves as AI strategy lead with dedicated title, but Notre Dame has not named a CAO.
Specific GPU count, node count, or named supercomputer for the Center for Research Computing were not publicly disclosed.
AWS grant dollar amount (credits) was not published; only in-kind credits are referenced.
No disclosed total investment envelope for the Data, AI, and Computing Initiative.
No evidence of active AI-related controversies or litigation as of April 2026 (controversies_and_litigation intentionally empty).
Precise launch dates for the SAI Initiative, AI Development Platform, and some Lucy Family Institute labs were not published.
Predictive-analytics-for-retention specific platforms at Notre Dame were not surfaced in public sources.
No specific published system-level AI policy applies (Notre Dame is private and non-system).

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: platform first.

products: ChatGPT Team / ChatGPT Edu (pilot to enterprise)

parent_vendors.vendor: Notre Dame OIT (internal) AI Development Platform (secure sandbox)
Praxis AI Praxis AI (classroom tutoring pilot)

public_claims.title: Generative AI Task Force (GAIT) and AI Innovation Council
public_claims.summary: Demonstrates bi-cameral governance (research + IT) combined with monthly operational cadence, a replicable model for mid-size private R1s.

public_claims.title: Enterprise Google Gemini and NotebookLM Rollout
public_claims.summary: Campus-wide deployment of enterprise generative AI at zero incremental license cost, reached through contractual data-protection terms — a template many private institutions are emulating.

public_claims.title: Scientific AI (SAI) Initiative
public_claims.summary: A domain-specific AI-for-science initiative with international collaboration and a funded graduate fellowship — the seed of a broader AI-for-discovery research cluster.

public_claims.title: Student Advising AI Chatbot Pilot
public_claims.summary: Classic enterprise-AI-for-student-services deployment built from validated data rather than public-web scraping.

public_claims.title: AI Development Platform (Internal Secure Sandbox)
public_claims.summary: Gives Notre Dame application developers a first-party platform rather than forcing them onto consumer APIs that can't hold institutional data.

public_claims.title: Privacy Sandbox (GPT-4 Persona-Based Consumer Research Tool)
public_claims.summary: A public-good AI research artifact aimed at non-academic end users, shipped as an open plugin.

public_claims.title: MS in Business Analytics (Mendoza, STEM-designated) and AI Coursework Portfolio
public_claims.summary: Provides the workforce-development layer of Notre Dame's AI strategy — a professional master's plus liberal-arts-friendly minors.

risk_readiness.rationale: Risk-bearing AI signals are paired with multiple public policy/control indicators.
